title: Firebase Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**Firebase** is Google's mobile/web app platform. This connector ‚Äî `FirebaseConnector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "firebase"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "service_account"`) ‚Äî wraps the five operational surfaces a Shielva tenant typically needs from a Firebase project:

| Surface | Base path | Capability |
|---|---|---|
| Firestore | `https://firestore.googleapis.com/v1/projects/{project_id}/databases/(default)/documents` | CRUD on documents in a collection |
| Realtime DB | `https://{database_name}.firebaseio.com` | Read / write a JSON tree by path |
| Identity Toolkit (Auth) | `https://identitytoolkit.googleapis.com/v1/projects/{project_id}/accounts` | List, get, create, update, delete users |
| FCM v1 | `https://fcm.googleapis.com/v1/projects/{project_id}/messages:send` | Send push notifications by token or topic |
| Cloud Storage | `https://firebasestorage.googleapis.com/v0/b/{bucket}/o` | List + upload objects in a Firebase Storage bucket |

The connector normalises Firestore documents and Identity Toolkit users into `NormalizedDocument` (id = `f"{tenant_id}_{source_id}"`), surfaces standalone `async def` methods per user-requested operation (OCP), retries 429/5xx with exponential backoff + jitter (3 attempts), and never embeds raw HTTP in `connector.py` (SOC ‚Äî all HTTP delegated to `client/http_client.py::FirebaseHTTPClient`).

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Generate a Firebase service-account JSON

1. Sign in to https://console.firebase.google.com.
2. Open the project you want to connect.
3. Click **Settings ‚Üí Project Settings ‚Üí Service Accounts**.
4. Click **Generate New Private Key** and confirm ‚Äî Firebase downloads a JSON file.
5. Open the JSON file; you'll see `project_id`, `client_email`, `private_key`, `token_uri`, etc.
6. (Optional) Find your **Realtime Database URL** under **Build ‚Üí Realtime Database** (looks like `https://<project>-default-rtdb.firebaseio.com`).
7. (Optional) Find your **Storage bucket** under **Build ‚Üí Storage** (defaults to `<project_id>.appspot.com`).

### 2. Install in ACP

In **ACP ‚Üí Manage Connectors ‚Üí Firebase**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `service_account_json` (secret, textarea) | Paste the entire JSON blob from step 5 |
| `database_url` (text, optional) | RTDB URL ‚Äî only needed for `get_realtime_db` / `set_realtime_db` |
| `storage_bucket` (text, optional) | Default Storage bucket; falls back to `{project_id}.appspot.com` |
| `scopes` (text, optional) | Override the OAuth2 scope list |
| `timeout_s` (number, optional) | Per-request httpx timeout, default 30 s |

Click **Install**. `install()` parses the JSON, signs an RS256 JWT, exchanges it at `https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token`, caches the access token, persists `project_id` + `client_email` + bucket defaults, and returns `ConnectorStatus(HEALTHY, AUTHENTICATED)`.

### 3. First health check

The gateway calls `health_check()` which probes a sentinel Firestore collection (`__shielva_health__`). On 2xx / 404 ‚Üí `HEALTHY + CONNECTED`. On 401 (bad credential) ‚Üí `OFFLINE + TOKEN_EXPIRED`. On 403 (service account lacks IAM role) ‚Üí `UNHEALTHY + INVALID_CREDENTIALS`. On 429 ‚Üí `DEGRADED + CONNECTED` with structured-log warning.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
Firebase admin REST APIs use the standard **Google service-account OAuth2 JWT-bearer** flow (`AUTH_TYPE = "service_account"`). No interactive consent screen ‚Äî the connector mints its own access token from the private key in the service-account JSON.

### Credential model

- `service_account_json` is the entire JSON downloaded from Firebase Console ‚Üí Service Accounts. The connector derives `project_id`, `client_email`, `private_key`, and `token_uri` from inside this blob ‚Äî so no separate `project_id` field is required.
- The JSON is treated as a secret: in production it must be sealed at rest (the Shielva platform stores it in the master-key-encrypted credential blob, not as plaintext on disk or in `.env`).
- `database_url` / `storage_bucket` are non-secret and persisted via `save_config`.

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Parses `service_account_json`. Signs RS256 JWT (`iss=client_email, scope=<scopes>, aud=token_uri, iat=now, exp=now+3600`). POSTs to `token_uri` with `grant_type=urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:jwt-bearer`. Caches the access token. Persists non-secret config. |
| `authorize(...)` | Returns a `TokenInfo` wrapping the cached access token. No interactive code exchange. |
| `health_check()` | Refreshes token if expired; probes `__shielva_health__` collection (404 OK). |
| `ensure_token()` | Handled internally by `FirebaseHTTPClient.get_access_token()` before every request ‚Äî `asyncio.Lock` guards concurrent mints. |
| token storage | In-process cache + `set_token(...)` from `install()`. Re-minted 60 s before expiry. |

### Header contract

Every outbound request to `*.googleapis.com` / `firebasestorage.googleapis.com`:

```
Authorization: Bearer <access_token>
Content-Type:   application/json
```

The service-account `private_key` never leaves the connector process ‚Äî only the short-lived bearer access token does.

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` ‚Äî never hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `service_account_json` | secret (textarea) | yes | `self._service_account` (parsed) | RS256 JWT signing material + project metadata |
| `database_url` | text | no | `self.database_url` | RTDB URL for `get_realtime_db` / `set_realtime_db` |
| `storage_bucket` | text | no | `self.storage_bucket` (fallback `{project_id}.appspot.com`) | Default Cloud Storage bucket |
| `scopes` | text | no | passed to `FirebaseHTTPClient(scope=...)` | OAuth2 scope override |
| `timeout_s` | number | no | `FirebaseHTTPClient(timeout=...)` | Per-request httpx timeout |
| `sync_collections` | array | no | `self.config['sync_collections']` | Firestore collections to ingest in `sync()` |
| `sync_auth_users` | boolean | no | `self.config['sync_auth_users']` | Page Identity Toolkit users in `sync()` |

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["service_account_json"]
_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("OFFLINE",   "TOKEN_EXPIRED"),
    403: ("UNHEALTHY", "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    429: ("DEGRADED",  "CONNECTED"),
}
DEFAULT_SCOPE = (
    "https://www.googleapis.com/auth/datastore "
    "https://www.googleapis.com/auth/firebase.database "
    "https://www.googleapis.com/auth/firebase.messaging "
    "https://www.googleapis.com/auth/identitytoolkit "
    "https://www.googleapis.com/auth/devstorage.read_write "
    "https://www.googleapis.com/auth/userinfo.email"
)
```

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `FirebaseConnector`. SOC: connector.py orchestrates only; HTTP lives in `client/http_client.py::FirebaseHTTPClient`; Firestore Value encode + Identity Toolkit user mapping live in `helpers/normalizer.py`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Parse `service_account_json` ‚Üí sign JWT ‚Üí exchange for access token ‚Üí persist derived config. Returns `HEALTHY + AUTHENTICATED` on success; `OFFLINE + MISSING_CREDENTIALS` when the JSON is missing/invalid; `OFFLINE + INVALID_CREDENTIALS` when the JWT exchange itself is rejected (e.g. private key revoked).

### `async authorize(auth_code, state) -> TokenInfo`
No-op for service-account auth ‚Äî returns the cached access token wrapped in a TokenInfo for callers that want to verify the credential is live without making a Firebase API call.

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
Mint / refresh token then probe `__shielva_health__`. 404 is treated as success. Failures classified via `_STATUS_MAP`.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
When `config.sync_collections` is set, pages each Firestore collection and ingests every document. When `config.sync_auth_users=True`, pages Identity Toolkit users. Otherwise returns `COMPLETED` empty.

children.title: Firestore
children.content:
### `async list_documents(collection, *, page_size=100, page_token=None) -> Dict`
`GET /documents/{collection}` ‚Äî cursor pagination via `pageToken`.

### `async get_document(collection, document_id) -> Dict`
`GET /documents/{collection}/{document_id}`.

### `async create_document(collection, fields, document_id=None) -> Dict`
`POST /documents/{collection}` ‚Äî optional `documentId` query param. Python `fields` are auto-encoded into Firestore Value envelopes (str ‚Üí `stringValue`, int ‚Üí `integerValue`, etc.).

### `async update_document(collection, document_id, fields) -> Dict`
`PATCH /documents/{collection}/{document_id}` ‚Äî same Value encoding.

### `async delete_document(collection, document_id) -> Dict`
`DELETE /documents/{collection}/{document_id}`.

children.title: Identity Toolkit (Firebase Auth admin)
children.content:
### `async list_users(*, page_size=1000, next_page_token=None) -> Dict`
`POST {identity_base}/accounts:batchGet` ‚Äî paginated.

### `async get_user(uid) -> Dict`
`POST {identity_base}/accounts:lookup` with body `{"localId": [uid]}`.

### `async create_user(email, password=None, display_name=None, phone_number=None) -> Dict`
`POST https://identitytoolkit.googleapis.com/v1/accounts`. The connector wraps the inputs in the `CreateUserRequest` pydantic model and serialises with `by_alias=True` so `display_name` ‚Üí `displayName` on the wire.

### `async update_user(uid, *, email=None, password=None, display_name=None, phone_number=None, disabled=None) -> Dict`
`POST {identity_base}/accounts:update` ‚Äî partial update keyed on `localId`. None-valued fields are omitted from the body.

### `async delete_user(uid) -> Dict`
`POST {identity_base}/accounts:delete` with body `{"localId": uid}`.

children.title: Cloud Messaging (FCM v1)
children.content:
### `async send_fcm_notification(token=None, topic=None, notification=None, data=None, android=None, apns=None) -> Dict`
`POST /v1/projects/{id}/messages:send`. Exactly one of `token` or `topic` must be provided ‚Äî the connector raises `ValueError` otherwise. None-valued message fields are stripped before send.

Example:

```python
await connector.send_fcm_notification(
    topic="news",
    notification={"title": "Headline", "body": "..."},
    data={"deep_link": "/articles/42"},
    android={"priority": "high"},
)
```

children.title: Realtime Database + Cloud Storage
children.content:
### `async get_realtime_db(path) -> Any`
`GET {database_url}/{path}.json`. Raises `FirebaseError` when `database_url` was not configured at install time.

### `async set_realtime_db(path, data) -> Any`
`PUT {database_url}/{path}.json` ‚Äî full overwrite at path. (Use `update_document` for Firestore-style merge semantics; RTDB's PATCH is intentionally not exposed here ‚Äî call the client directly if you need it.)

### `async list_storage_objects(*, bucket=None, prefix=None, page_size=100, page_token=None) -> Dict`
`GET https://firebasestorage.googleapis.com/v0/b/{bucket}/o`. Defaults to the install-field `storage_bucket` (or `{project_id}.appspot.com`).

### `async upload_storage_object(name, data, *, content_type="application/octet-stream", bucket=None) -> Dict`
`POST .../o?name={name}&uploadType=media` ‚Äî body is the raw object bytes; `Content-Type` reflects `content_type`.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
FirebaseError                              # base; carries status_code + response_body
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ FirebaseAuthError                      # 401 / 403
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ FirebaseBadRequestError                # 400
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ FirebaseNotFoundError                  # 404
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ FirebaseConflictError                  # 409 (duplicate email, write conflict)
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ FirebaseRateLimitError                 # 429 - retry_after_s
‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ FirebaseServerError                    # 5xx
```

Back-compat aliases preserved:
```
FirebaseNetworkError = FirebaseServerError   # legacy name from older code
FirebaseNotFound     = FirebaseNotFoundError
```

### Retry behaviour (`client/http_client.py::_request`)

| Status | Action |
|---|---|
| 400 / 401 / 403 / 404 / 409 | Raise immediately |
| 429 | Exponential backoff `_BASE_BACKOFF_S * 2 ** attempt` (0.5 s, 1 s, 2 s) + 0-250 ms jitter, retry up to `_MAX_RETRIES=3` |
| 5xx | Same exponential backoff, retry up to 3 |
| `httpx.HTTPError` (timeout / connect / read) | Backoff, retry up to 3, then raise `FirebaseNetworkError` |

### How errors surface to the gateway

`health_check()` catches exceptions and maps via `_STATUS_MAP`:

```
401 -> ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE,   TOKEN_EXPIRED)
403 -> ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)
429 -> ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  CONNECTED)   - logged as warning
other -> ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, CONNECTED)
```

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install - message mentions `not valid JSON` | The textarea got the secret pasted with stray characters (Markdown fences, trailing commas) | Re-download the JSON from Firebase Console; paste verbatim. |
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install - message mentions `missing required fields` | The JSON blob is missing `client_email`, `private_key`, or `project_id` | Regenerate the service account key - Firebase always emits all three. |
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` at install | The OAuth2 token endpoint returned 401 - the key is revoked or the time on the connector pod is skewed > 5 minutes | Rotate the service-account key in Firebase Console; verify host clock via NTP. |
| `ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` at health_check | 403 from Firestore - the service account lacks the right IAM role | In GCP Console -> IAM, grant the SA `roles/datastore.user`, `roles/firebaseauth.admin`, `roles/firebasenotifications.admin`, `roles/firebasedatabase.admin`, `roles/storage.objectAdmin` as needed. |
| `firebase.http.retry` warnings spike | Hitting 429 (Firebase per-project quota) | The client already retries with exponential backoff. Lower call rate or batch with Firestore commits. |
| 404 on `get_document` / `delete_document` for an existing doc | Wrong collection (case-sensitive!) or you used the document path including `/` segments | Double-check the collection name and pass the bare document id (the SDK builds the full path). |
| `FirebaseError: database_url is not configured` on RTDB call | Operator left the install field blank | Re-install with the RTDB URL (`https://<project>-default-rtdb.firebaseio.com`). |
| `FirebaseError: storage_bucket is not configured` on storage call | `storage_bucket` blank AND the SA JSON had no `project_id` | Set `storage_bucket` explicitly (`<project>.appspot.com`). |
| FCM 404 `Requested entity was not found` | Device token expired (Android FCM tokens rotate) | Refresh the device token from the client app and re-send. |
| `ImportError: cannot import name 'FirebaseNetworkError'` | Older `exceptions.py` without back-compat aliases | Already fixed - `FirebaseNetworkError = FirebaseServerError` alias retained in `exceptions.py`. |
